programmes.show_description: When Gran is at death's door, Alf is sure she wants him to take her valuable watch - to remember her by, of course. : Gran's Watch
With Freddie Mercury, Gary Numan, The Shadows and The Pretenders. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy extravaganza from Thames TV (1980) - and all in the best possible taste!
The elusive High Fence is still at large; an arrested suspect has been poisoned to avoid him talking. Simon aims to find out what has happened and who the High Fence is. S2 E23: The High Fence
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!

Examining the history of war tactics. Strategic bombing takes war to the heartland of the enemy. It seeks to destroy not only an enemy's ability to wage war, but also its will to fight on. S2 E3
Discover the real stories of secret operations and spies behind enemy lines in the Gulf War, including a German undercover agent who worked for Iraq. S2 E3: The Gulf War - Secrets in the Sand
The definitive combat helicopter saw first blood in South East Asia and has been transport, gunship, smoke ship, command centre, MediVac and dozens of other roles. S2 E5
A look at how the West End became a place for the wealthy elite, the impact of the railways, a cholera epidemic and engineer Joseph Bazalgette saving London with his new sewerage system. Ep3
The soldiers whose name has become a synonym for heroism and tackling the seemingly impossible; what was the task of the SAS, Britain's elite military force, in World War II? Ep3: SAS
The evolution of the Vietnam conflict from a regional military engagement to an ever-expanding war, here exploring how it was logistically possible and a look at the Huey helicopter and its role.
As Britain began to strike Nazi Germany, it was soon suffering losses from hi-tech air defences; in 1941, British paratroops were sent to seize a German radar system which needed to be cracked.
When it was impossible for aircraft to land safely on enemy territory, gliders were the perfect answer for delivering special forces behind enemy lines. Ep3: Gliders

The US Air Force finds itself flying planes older than the pilots - where do the strategic bomber, and its little brother the fighter bomber, fit into 21st-century warfare? E3: The High Ground vs. SAM
Armed with the latest undersea technology and personal testimony, we board two doomed ships to discover what set them on the path to catastrophe. Ep2: Danger Ahead
With Boomtown Rats. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy extravaganza from Thames TV (1980). All in the best possible taste!
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S6, E6: A Question Of Taste
It's the autumn of 1908 as Sarah returns while James and Elizabeth are helping the poor at a refuge; soon brought back to the house, Sarah stirs up trouble for all. S1 E10: A Voice From The Past
Arthur sells some goods to a Russian sailor when a ship arrives in port, but he swiftly finds himself involved in a diplomatic situation - and much worse off. S6 E5: From Fulham With Love
Extortion and blackmail lead to a man being murdered, an old friend of Makepeace's being badly injured, and Makepeace taking Dempsey home. S2 E5: Tequila Sunrise
Drama series. Simon Neary is a gangster that Gene has wanted to nail for years, but is stifling his gun deal a way to keep Alex from getting shot in 2008? S1 E5
Alf is feeling the effects of the demon drink. Else is unsure whether it was the single glass of port or the sausages that made him ill. S5 E6: Party Night
With Boomtown Rats and Suzi Quatro. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy extravaganza from Thames TV (1980). All in the best possible taste!
Simon's holiday to a Greek village is interrupted when a mysterious visitor returns from America and The Saint must step in when they cause trouble for the locals. S2 E24: Sophia

Examining the history of war tactics. Flank attack - going around the side of the enemy - is a basic manoeuvre, yet stories of victories decided by flanking fill military textbooks. S2 E4
From supersonic spy planes to sophisticated satellites, get an insider's look at the pioneers of aerial reconnaissance and the profound impact they have had on modern warfare. S2 E4: Spies in the Sky
The F-15 Eagle immediately turned the tide of the first Gulf War to prove its greatness, and was involved in one of the most bizarre combat incidents during that conflict. S2 E6
An exploration of London's underground railway and skyline, the origins of Metro-land - London's vast suburbia - and the clever marketing that tempted millions to move outside the city. Ep4
We look at the British Armoured Division, known as the Desert Rats, who conducted an epic campaign against Rommel's Axis forces in the deserts of Egypt and Libya. Ep4: Desert Rats
We examine the Vietnam War from the air, water and land along with those that fought look at the importance of reconnaissance in the war effort and the role of the American navy in Vietnam.
Ordered to attack the St Nazaire U-boat base, British commandos undertook 'the greatest raid of all' by turning an old destroyer into a live bomb to ram the gates. Ep4: Storm at St. Nazaire
Giant aircraft carriers dominated the Pacific War between Japan and the USA - Pearl Harbor was launched to destroy US carriers but failed. Ep4: Aircraft Carriers
The Supermarine Spitfire aircraft begged the question of how important air combat is in WWII - it has been clear that dominating the skies is essential in battle since then. E4: Pure Fighter
Even the most powerful WWII warships had their weaknesses - we see a North Atlantic search for HMS Hood and the German battleship HMS Bismarck. Ep3: Last Days of the Battleship
With Suzi Quatro and Joe Jackson. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy extravaganza from Thames TV (1980). All in the best possible taste!
Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S6, E7: A Pair Of Bloomers
October 1908: there is a thief in the house, but who is the culprit? Is it Sarah, a visiting friend of James, or perhaps something more organised than just petty theft? S1 E11: The Swedish Tiger
Arthur is only too willing to help when local eccentric Albert comes into an inheritance - but his help isn't necessarily needed. S6 E6: Waiting For Goddard
The duo probe the connections between a murdered antiques dealer and an Arab embassy official with diplomatic immunity after a robbery. S2 E6: Blood Money
Alex thinks she's close to death and has to keep her brain alive by solving a raid at a Post Office; Gene believes the culprit is Chas Cale, a blagger he once crossed. S1 E6
